channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: War and neglect fuel deadly measles epidemic in Sudan’s Darfur
item.description: The measles epidemic in East Darfur's Labado has killed 70 people in a few weeks, with healthcare collapse leaving families without medicines. The region's healthcare system has been severely neglected.

item.title: Trump Lashes Out At Reporter For "Reading Shooter's Crap"
item.description: President Trump responded to a shooting by blaming Democratic leaders for "hate speech" that he claimed contributed to the violence. He made the comment while addressing the media. The president blamed "hate speech" by Democratic leaders for the political violence in America.

item.title: India's MobiKwik gets central bank nod for non-banking financial company licence
item.description: India's MobiKwik has received a non-banking financial company licence from the central bank. The licence will allow MobiKwik to expand its financial services. India's MobiKwik gets central bank nod for non-banking financial company licence.

item.title: Dollar steady as traders eye US-Iran talks, central banks
item.description: The US dollar has remained steady as traders watch US-Iran talks and central bank actions. Market sentiment is cautious ahead of key economic events. Dollar steady as traders eye US-Iran talks, central banks.

item.title: Tired And Worried, Seafarers Have Been Stranded In Persian Gulf For Weeks
item.description: Dozens of ships have been stranded in the Persian Gulf for weeks, with seafarers reporting fatigue and worry. The ships have come under attack since the war started. Dozens of ships have come under attack since the war started.

item.title: Iran War Disrupts The Circuit Board Supply Chain, Raises Costs For Tech Firms
item.description: The Iran war has disrupted the global circuit board supply chain, raising costs for tech firms. The global PCB industry is projected to increase by 12.5% to $95.8 billion in 2026.

item.title: Trump Event Shooting Highlights: Accused Shooter Likely Targeted Trump And His Top Officials
item.description: Donald Trump was evacuated after gunshots were heard at his event in the US. Trump event shooting Live Updates: Donald Trump was evacuated after gunshots were heard at his event.

item.title: Iran war deadline heats up Trump-Congress showdown
item.description: US President Trump faces a May 1 deadline to seek congressional approval for military action against Iran, as the 60-day limit for operations without approval expires. Congress is likely to scrutinize any request for a formal declaration of war.

item.title: ‘I have to protect them’: The man guarding Mauritania’s rare Islamic books
item.description: In Mauritania, a guardian protects the rare Islamic books of Chinguetti, a desert town once famous for its library collections that drew scholars from the Muslim world. The town's historical significance relies on preserving these precious manuscripts.

item.title: ‘Kindness is in the Philippines' DNA’: Communities offer meals, aid to drivers hit by soaring fuel
item.description: In the Philippines, communities are offering meals and aid to drivers affected by soaring fuel prices. There are growing calls for the government to provide more concrete support, including increased subsidies for drivers.

item.title: ACL injury ends Xavi Simons’ World Cup 2026 dream with the Netherlands
item.description: Dutch midfielder Xavi Simons' World Cup 2026 aspirations have ended due to a suspected ACL injury to his right knee. The injury eliminates him from contention for the FIFA World Cup with the Netherlands.

item.title: Adidas shares pop after beating Nike to sub-two-hour marathon milestone
item.description: Adidas shares rose after its athletes achieved a sub-two-hour marathon milestone, beating rival Nike. This performance boost comes as Adidas aims to strengthen its position in the competitive sportswear market.

item.title: Russian drone attack on Odesa wounds 10, officials say
item.description: A Russian drone attack on Odesa damaged port infrastructure, including an energy facility within a cargo terminal, wounding 10 people. The attack targeted Black Sea port infrastructure. Russia struck ​port infrastructure on ‌the Black ​Sea ⁠in Odesa, which damaged ​an ‌energy facility within the cargo terminal.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: ‘Awaiting orders’, Iran says some forces yet to join war as
item.description: Iranian lawmaker pushes Hormuz toll plan as US-Israel peace talks stall with Iran. US-Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: Iranian lawmaker pushes Hormuz toll plan as peace talks stall The Times of India.

item.title: Zaragoza goalkeeper punches player, sparks brawl in La Liga 2 derby match
item.description: Real Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada punched Huesca's Jorge Pulido, sparking a brawl in a La Liga 2 derby match, and faces a ban after being shown a red card. Real Zaragoza's Esteban Andrada faces a ban for punching Huesca's Jorge Pulido after being shown a red card.

item.title: Nissan raises fiscal 2025 operating profit forecast
item.description: Nissan raised its fiscal 2025 operating profit forecast. The new forecast was not specified. Nissan raises fiscal 2025 operating profit forecast.

item.title: Iran's Big Warning To Gulf Nations After Trump's Threats
item.description: Iran responded to US President Trump's threats, highlighting its advantages in the standoff. Iranian official Ghalibaf made the comments. Ghalibaf also responded to Trump's threat, highlighting Iran's advantage in the standoff.

item.title: UN Experts Visit Southern Taiwan to Examine 'Taiwan Model' for Net-Zero Transition and Biocultural
item.description: UN experts visited southern Taiwan to examine the 'Taiwan Model' for net-zero transition and biocultural governance. The visit aimed to study Taiwan's approach. UN Experts Visit Southern Taiwan to Examine 'Taiwan Model' for Net-Zero Transition and Biocultural Governance The National Law Review.

item.title: U.S. says three killed in boat strike as campaign toll hits 185
item.description: The US military's Southern Command reported that a boat strike killed three people, with intelligence confirming the vessel was operated by designated terrorist organisations and transiting narco-trafficking routes. The incident brings the campaign toll to 185.

item.title: Oil prices rise as US-Iran peace talks stall
item.description: Oil prices rose as US-Iran peace talks stalled, following US President Trump's announcement that plans to send a team to Pakistan for negotiations had been cancelled. The development impacted global oil markets.
